business_problem: Shopify powers millions of merchants in more than 175 countries whose traffic can spike within seconds — particularly during Black Friday and Cyber Monday — and traditional static environments would have required costly overprovisioning to survive peak demand without compromising reliability for 81M buyers.
solution_summary: Shopify's commerce platform processes record-breaking transaction volumes on Google Cloud during Black Friday weekend 2025, handling $14.6B in global sales and 489M requests per minute at peak. Compute Engine runs general-purpose commerce services on N2/N4 machines, C4 instances power Shop App catalog search, and Z-series machines provide high-performance local storage for real-time package tracking across 136M deliveries. Google Kubernetes Engine orchestrates auto-scaling microservices to absorb traffic spikes instantly. Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme store databases supporting 14.8T queries and 1.75T writes during the weekend. AI workloads leverage accelerator-optimized VMs to power Shopify Magic recommendations and Sidekick assistant features.
business_outcome: Shopify absorbed record peak commerce traffic without overprovisioning, giving merchants confidence that flash sales, viral campaigns and global expansion are no longer bounded by platform reliability.
metrics: $14.6B in global Black Friday-Cyber Monday weekend sales (27% YoY)
489M requests per minute at peak traffic
2.2 trillion edge requests over the weekend
136M packages tracked globally via the Shop App
architecture_clues: Compute Engine N2 and N4 for general-purpose core commerce, C4 for Shop App catalogue search
Distributed global database layer on Compute Engine Z-series machines with high-performance local storage
Microservices on GKE for instant elastic scaling during traffic spikes
Custom machine types tuning CPU-to-memory ratio per workload
Persistent Disk for general storage; Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme for high-performance databases
Accelerator-optimized VMs powering Shopify Magic and Sidekick AI assistant
Classic compute workloads on GKE call AI services through internal APIs; Hyperdisk provides high-throughput delivery
evidence_quotes: Processed $14.6B in global sales, a 27% increase YoY, during Black Friday weekend
489M requests per minute at peak traffic
We chose Google because we needed a partner who could understand and support hyper-scale reliability engineering.
Shopify has also built a distributed global-scale database layer on Compute Engine's Z-series machines, which provide high-performance local storage.
We used Google's accelerator-optimized VMs to take these workloads from PoC to production.

solution_components.name: Compute Engine
solution_components.role: Runs core commerce services on N2/N4 general-purpose machines, C4 compute-optimized machines for search, and Z-series storage-optimized machines for distributed database layers.

solution_components.name: Google Kubernetes Engine
solution_components.role: Orchestrates containerized microservices with instant horizontal auto-scaling to handle traffic spikes from 0 to 489M requests/minute during peak demand.

solution_components.name: Persistent Disk
solution_components.role: Provides standard block storage for general application workloads and middleware components.

solution_components.name: Hyperdisk Balanced
solution_components.role: Delivers high-throughput, balanced performance storage for transactional databases supporting order processing and customer data.

solution_components.name: Hyperdisk Extreme
solution_components.role: Enables ultra-high-performance databases for real-time inventory and package tracking handling 14.8T queries and 1.75T writes during peak shopping periods.

data_flow: Customer purchase requests → N2/N4 Compute Engine processing → checkout service updates order database on Hyperdisk Extreme → payment processing → inventory deduction → Z-series machines track 136M packages globally in real-time via Shop App. Recommendation queries route to C4 machines for catalog search. AI inference for Shopify Magic and Sidekick routes to accelerator-optimized VMs. All responses aggregated through GKE service mesh.
